title: Mimosopsis imparilis
taxonomy: Mimosopsis imparilis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, with the family Mimosaceae, and the subfamily Mimosoideae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is also known to be part of the genus Mimosopsis. However, some sources may categorize it as a synonym of Pithecellobium imparile. Further research is needed to confirm its classification with certainty.

The plant's sap is rich in waxes and has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
